Name: Petilil
Flavor-Text:
The leaves on its head grow right back even if they fall out.
These bitter leaves refresh those who eat them.

Name: Lilligant
Flavor-Text: The fragrance of the garland on its head has a relaxing effect, but taking care of it is very difficult.

Name: Rowlet
Effects:
Flip 3 coins.
This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text:
It sends its feathers, which are as sharp as blades, flying in attack.
Its legs are strong, so its kicks are also formidable.

Name: Dartrix
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
This narcissistic Pokémon is a clean freak.
If you don’t groom it diligently, it may stop listening to you.

Name: Decidueye
Effects: This attack does 70 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
It nocks its arrow quills and shoots them at opponents.
When it simply can’t afford to miss, it tugs the vine on its head to improve its focus.

Name: Buzzwole ex
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Big Beat.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Pheromosa
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A life-form that lives in another world, its body is thin and supple, but it also possesses great power.

Name: Kartana
Flavor-Text: This Ultra Beast’s body, which is as thin as paper, is like a sharpened sword.

Name: Blacephalon
Effects: This Pokémon also does 70 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text:
It slithers toward people.
Then, without warning, it triggers the explosion of its own head.
It’s apparently one kind of Ultra Beast.

Name: Mantine
Flavor-Text: If it builds up enough speed swimming, it can jump out above the waves and glide for over 300 feet.

Name: Carvanha
Flavor-Text:
It won’t attack while it’s alone—not even if it spots prey.
Instead, it waits for other Carvanha to join it, and then the Pokémon attack as a group.

Name: Sharpedo
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: As soon as it catches the scent of prey, Sharpedo will jet seawater from its backside, hurtling toward the target to attack at 75 mph.

Name: Shinx
Flavor-Text:
The extension and contraction of its muscles generates electricity.
It glows when in trouble.

Name: Luxio
Flavor-Text:
Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws.
A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

Name: Luxray
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, attacks used by your opponent's Active Pokémon do −20 damage.
Flavor-Text: It can see clearly through walls to track down its prey and seek its lost young.

Name: Blitzle
Flavor-Text:
When thunderclouds cover the sky, it will appear.
It can catch lightning with its mane and store the electricity.

Name: Zebstrika
Flavor-Text: When this ill-tempered Pokémon runs wild, it shoots lightning from its mane in all directions.

Name: Emolga
Flavor-Text:
As Emolga flutters through the air, it crackles with electricity.
This Pokémon is cute, but it can cause a lot of trouble.

Name: Tapu Koko ex
Effects: Take a [L] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Xurkitree
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Although it’s alien to this world and a danger here, it’s apparently a common organism in the world where it normally lives.

Name: Zeraora
Ability-Effect: At the end of your first turn, take a [L] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
It electrifies its claws and tears its opponents apart with them.
Even if they dodge its attack, they’ll be electrocuted by the flying sparks.

Name: Clefairy
Flavor-Text: It is said that happiness will come to those who see a gathering of Clefairy dancing under a full moon.

Name: Clefable
Flavor-Text: A timid fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen, it will run and hide the moment it senses people.

Name: Phantump
Flavor-Text: With a voice like a human child’s, it cries out to lure adults deep into the forest, getting them lost among the trees.

Name: Trevenant
Flavor-Text: People fear it due to a belief that it devours any who try to cut down trees in its forest, but to the Pokémon it shares its woods with, it’s kind.

Name: Morelull
Flavor-Text:
It scatters spores that flicker and glow.
Anyone seeing these lights falls into a deep slumber.

Name: Shiinotic
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may put a random Pokémon from your deck into your hand.
Flavor-Text:
Its flickering spores lure in prey and put them to sleep.
Once this Pokémon has its prey snoozing, it drains their vitality with its fingertips.

Name: Meditite
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text:
It never skips its daily yoga training.
It heightens its inner strength through meditation.

Name: Medicham
Flavor-Text: Through yoga training, it gained the psychic power to predict its foe’s next move.

Name: Baltoy
Flavor-Text:
It moves while spinning around on its single foot.
Some Baltoy have been seen spinning on their heads.

Name: Claydol
Ability-Effect: Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) can't be healed.
Flavor-Text: This mysterious Pokémon started life as an ancient clay figurine made over 20,000 years ago.

Name: Rockruff
Flavor-Text:
This Pokémon is very friendly when it’s young.
Its disposition becomes vicious once it matures, but it never forgets the kindness of its master.

Name: Lycanroc ex
Effects: Discard a [F] Energy from this Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Passimian
Effects: If Passimian is on your Bench, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text:
This Pokémon battles by throwing hard berries.
It won’t obey a Trainer who throws Poké Balls without skill.

Name: Sandygast
Flavor-Text: If it loses its shovel, it will stick something else—like a branch—in its head to make do until it finds another shovel.

Name: Palossand
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.
Flavor-Text:
The terrifying Palossand drags smaller Pokémon into its sandy body.
Once its victims are trapped, it drains them of their vitality whenever it pleases.

Name: Alolan Meowth
Effects: Discard a random Pokémon Tool card from your opponent's hand.
Flavor-Text:
It’s accustomed to luxury because it used to live with Alolan royalty.
As a result, it’s very picky about food.

Name: Alolan Persian
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: The round face of Alolan Persian is considered to be a symbol of prosperity in the Alola region, so these Pokémon are very well cared for.

Name: Sandile
Flavor-Text:
It submerges itself in sand and moves as if swimming.
This wise behavior keeps its enemies from finding it and maintains its temperature.

Name: Krokorok
Flavor-Text:
Protected by thin membranes, their eyes can see even in the dead of night.
They live in groups of a few individuals.

Name: Krookodile
Effects:
Flip 3 coins.
For each heads, a card is chosen at random from your opponent's hand.
Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.
Flavor-Text: After clamping down with its powerful jaws, it twists its body around to rip its prey in half.

Name: Nihilego
Ability-Effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon takes +10 damage from being Poisoned.
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: A life-form from another world, it was dubbed a UB and is thought to produce a strong neurotoxin.

Name: Guzzlord ex
Effects:
Flip a coin until you get tails.
For each heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Poipole
Effects:
Flip 2 coins.
This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: This Ultra Beast is well enough liked to be chosen as a first partner in its own world.

Name: Naganadel
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text:
It stores hundreds of liters of poisonous liquid inside its body.
It is one of the organisms known as UBs.

Name: Alolan Diglett
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: The metal-rich geology of this Pokémon’s habitat caused it to develop steel whiskers on its head.

Name: Alolan Dugtrio ex
Effects:
Flip 3 coins.
This attack does 60 damage for each heads.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Aron
Flavor-Text: It eats iron ore—and sometimes railroad tracks— to build up the steel armor that protects its body.

Name: Lairon
Flavor-Text:
It loves iron ore.
Groups of them fight for territory by bashing one another with their steel bodies.

Name: Aggron
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.
Flavor-Text:
Aggron has a horn sharp enough to perforate thick iron sheets.
It brings down its opponents by ramming into them horn first.

Name: Ferroseed
Flavor-Text:
It absorbs the iron it finds in the rock while clinging to the ceiling.
It shoots spikes when in danger.

Name: Ferrothorn
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, do 20 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon scrapes its spikes across rocks, and then uses the tips of its feelers to absorb the nutrients it finds within the stone.

Name: Stakataka
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text:
It appeared from an Ultra Wormhole.
Each one appears to be made up of many life-forms stacked one on top of each other.

Name: Lillipup
Flavor-Text:
This Pokémon is courageous but also cautious.
It uses the soft fur covering its face to collect information about its surroundings.

Name: Herdier
Flavor-Text:
Herdier is a very smart and friendly Pokémon.
So much so that there’s a theory that Herdier was the first Pokémon to partner with people.

Name: Stoutland
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, attacks used by your opponent's Active Pokémon cost 1 [C] more.
Flavor-Text:
These Pokémon seem to enjoy living with humans.
Even a Stoutland caught in the wild will warm up to people in about three days.

Name: Stufful
Flavor-Text: Its fluffy fur is a delight to pet, but carelessly reaching out to touch this Pokémon could result in painful retaliation.

Name: Bewear
Effects:
Flip 2 coins.
If both of them are heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Flavor-Text:
Once it accepts you as a friend, it tries to show its affection with a hug.
Letting it do that is dangerous—it could easily shatter your bones.

Name: Oranguru
Flavor-Text:
It normally spends its time meditating in the treetops.
It throws Poké Balls and gives other Pokémon orders as it pleases.

Name: Type: Null
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon weapon developed for a specific mission, it went berserk during an experiment, so it was cryogenically frozen.

Name: Silvally
Effects: If you played a Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text:
This is its form once it has awakened and evolved.
Freed from its heavy mask, its speed is greatly increased.

Name: Celesteela
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may switch your Active Ultra Beast with 1 of your Benched Ultra Beasts.
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: One of the dangerous UBs, high energy readings can be detected coming from both of its huge arms.

Name: Beast Wall
Description: You can use this card only if your opponent hasn't gotten any points.During your opponent's next turn, all of your Ultra Beasts take −20 damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon.

Name: Repel
Description: Switch out your opponent's Active Basic Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

Name: Electrical Cord
Description: If the [L] Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, move 2 [L] Energy from that Pokémon and attach 1 Energy each to 2 of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Beastite
Description: Attacks used by the Ultra Beast this card is attached to do +10 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon for each point you have gotten.

Name: Gladion
Description: Put 1 random Type: Null or Silvally from your deck into your hand.

Name: Looker
Description: Your opponent reveals all of the Supporter cards in their deck.

Name: Lusamine
Description: You can use this card only if your opponent has gotten at least 1 point.Choose 1 of your Ultra Beasts. Attach 2 random Energy from your discard pile to that Pokémon.

Name: Rowlet
Effects:
Flip 3 coins.
This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text:
It sends its feathers, which are as sharp as blades, flying in attack.
Its legs are strong, so its kicks are also formidable.

Flavor-Text: A life-form that lives in another world, its body is thin and supple, but it also possesses great power.

Flavor-Text:

Then, without warning, it triggers the explosion of its own head.
It’s apparently one kind of Ultra Beast.

Effects: Discard a random Pokémon Tool card from your opponent's hand.
Flavor-Text:
It’s accustomed to luxury because it used to live with Alolan royalty.
As a result, it’s very picky about food.

Effects: Take a [L] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Lycanroc ex
Effects: Discard a [F] Energy from this Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Guzzlord ex
Effects:
Flip a coin until you get tails.
For each heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Alolan Dugtrio ex
Effects:
Flip 3 coins.
This attack does 60 damage for each heads.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Buzzwole ex
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Big Beat.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Growlithe
Flavor-Text:
It has a brave and trustworthy nature.
It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.

Name: Arcanine
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: An ancient picture scroll shows that people were captivated by its movement as it ran through prairies.

Name: Froakie
Flavor-Text:
It secretes flexible bubbles from its chest and back.
The bubbles reduce the damage it would otherwise take when attacked.

Name: Frogadier
Flavor-Text: It can throw bubble-covered pebbles with precise  control, hitting empty cans up to a hundred feet away.

Name: Greninja
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may do 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
It creates throwing stars out of compressed water.
 When it spins them and throws them at high speed,  these stars can split metal in two.

Name: Jynx
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
Its strange cries sound like human language.
There are some musicians who compose songs for Jynx to sing.

Name: Pidgey
Flavor-Text:
A common sight in forests and woods.
It flaps its wings at ground level to kick up blinding sand.

Name: Pidgeotto
Flavor-Text:
The claws on its feet are well developed.
It can carry prey such as an Exeggcute to its nest over 60 miles away.

Name: Pidgeot
Ability-Effect:
Once during your turn, you may switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench.
(Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: When hunting, it skims the surface of water at high speed to pick off unwary prey such as Magikarp.

Name: Aerodactyl
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, your opponent shuffles their Active Pokémon back into their deck.
Flavor-Text:
This is a ferocious Pokémon from ancient times.
Apparently even modern technology is incapable of producing a perfectly restored specimen.

Name: Celebi ex
Effects:
Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon.
This attack does 50 damage for each heads.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Arcanine ex
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Aerodactyl ex
Ability-Effect: Your opponent can't play any Pokémon from their hand to evolve their Active Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Pidgeot ex
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Nihilego
Ability-Effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon takes +10 damage from being Poisoned.
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: A life-form from another world, it was dubbed a UB and is thought to produce a strong neurotoxin.
